response.justification: Sarah Smith has a strong background in software development with experience in C++ (Node.js/C++ mix at National Instruments) and Rust (not explicitly listed but comparable to Dlang, which she has used extensively). However, her primary focus has been on web, mobile, and embedded systems with languages like TypeScript, Node.js, and Dlang, not geospatial or routing systems. Her geospatial experience (GISCollective) is relevant but lacks depth in routing data pipelines or navigation systems. The mismatch in core technologies and domain expertise reduces her overall fit for this role.
strengths: Experience with C++ (National Instruments, robotics projects) and systems programming (Dlang, low-latency apps at Dunnhumby).
Proven ability to optimize performance and reduce latency in real-time systems.
Strong problem-solving skills and willingness to expand technical expertise.
concerns: Limited direct experience with routing systems, geospatial data processing for navigation, or modern C++ development in commercial navigation pipelines.
Primary expertise in web/mobile development (TypeScript, Node.js) rather than low-level systems or distributed routing systems.
No clear evidence of experience with Rust or geospatial data transformation for routing tiles.
